feature_title: Improve Search and Re-evaluate Subscription Pricing
title: Fix Search Quality and Address Pricing Friction
summary: Search filters silently fail and recipe matching is unreliable, pushing power users to cancel. Combined with $15/mo perceived as steep, this cluster drives the highest churn risk across our corpus. This PRD scopes the search fixes and a pricing-experiment track.

content.problem: Across 10 distinct feedback items, users report that search returns irrelevant results (dessert recipes when querying 'pasta'), filters do not apply (vegan + under-30-min still returns steak dishes), and saved recipes can be missing from search results entirely. The same cluster surfaces strong pricing pushback: $15/mo is described as 'too expensive for a recipe app' by paying users, several of whom mention cancelling. The two themes co-occur because pricing tolerance collapses when core search is broken — users only notice the cost when the product feels low-value.
content.goal: Restore search reliability to a measurable baseline (>90% query-success rate) and validate whether the current $15/mo price point is the actual churn driver — within 8 weeks.
user_stories: As a free-tier user evaluating Pro, I want search filters to actually filter so that I can trust the product before paying.
As a Pro subscriber, I want to find recipes I have saved so that my collection is reliable.
As a vegan user, I want the vegan filter to exclude all animal products so that I don't have to manually verify every result.
As a price-sensitive user, I want clearer feature/value framing during the upgrade flow so that the $15 price feels justified.
success_metrics: Query success rate (clicks within top-3 results / total queries) >= 90% within 30 days of search-fix launch (baseline: ~62%).
Search-related support tickets down 50% within 30 days of launch.
Cancellation rate citing 'search' or 'price' in exit survey down 25% within 60 days.
Pricing A/B test: incremental revenue per visitor at $12/mo vs $15/mo, measured over 4 weeks with 80% statistical power.
requirements: Fix filter intersection bug: vegan + time + cuisine filters must AND together, not OR.
Fix saved-recipe indexing: any recipe in the user's 'Saved' collection must appear when searched by name with <=1 character typo (Levenshtein <= 1).
Fix dietary classification: vegan filter must exclude any recipe whose ingredient list contains items from the maintained 'animal_products' taxonomy (>=120 entries).
Search latency: P95 query response time <= 800ms on Android (current P95 is 5.1s — see related cluster 'Slow Android Search Performance').
Pricing experiment: implement traffic-split assignment ($12/$15) at signup, persisted per user, with a kill-switch under feature-flag pricing.experiment.v1.
Exit survey: add 'reason for cancelling' multi-select with options including 'too expensive', 'search broken', 'missing features', 'other'.
edge_cases: User has filters active from a previous session — should they persist or reset on app open? (Decision: reset; document in changelog.)
Vegan filter + an ingredient query like 'butter' — return vegan-butter alternatives only, with a banner explaining the substitution.
Saved recipe deleted from source DB but still in user's Saved list — search should return it with a 'no longer available' badge, not silently drop.
User in pricing experiment churns and rejoins — must land in the same price cohort to avoid revenue contamination.
Free-tier user hits search 100+ times/day — apply rate limit and surface upsell, do not silently slow them down.
out_of_scope: Recipe recommendation engine improvements (covered by 'Recipe accuracy' PRD).
Annual subscription pricing — pricing experiment is monthly-tier only for this round.
Localization of the exit survey — English-only for v1, ship i18n in a follow-up.

critique_notes.message: The 'query success rate' baseline is given as approximately 62% but the methodology for measuring it is not specified.
critique_notes.suggestion: Define query-success as: a user clicks one of the top-3 results within 30s of submitting a query, AND does not refine the query within 60s. Measure on backend logs, not client-side, to avoid bias.

critique_notes.message: 'Maintained animal_products taxonomy (>=120 entries)' — unclear who maintains it and how it's updated.
critique_notes.suggestion: Add: 'Taxonomy is owned by the Content team and reviewed quarterly; updates are PR-reviewed by the on-call PM.'

critique_notes.message: Last user story ('clearer feature/value framing') is vague — what specifically gets clearer?
critique_notes.suggestion: Replace with: 'As a price-sensitive user, I want to see a comparison of free-tier vs Pro features on the upgrade screen, with the 3 features I have used most this week highlighted, so the price feels tied to value I have experienced.'

critique_notes.message: 'Free-tier user hits search 100+ times/day — apply rate limit and surface upsell.' What is the rate limit threshold? What does 'surface upsell' look like?
critique_notes.suggestion: Specify: 'After the 100th search of the day on free tier, return a soft-gate modal with the Pro upgrade CTA; subsequent searches return results but with the upsell banner pinned for the rest of the day.'

_meta.note: Representative example output captured from a prior live run. Structurally identical to what backend/app/agents/prd_generator.py produces; critique_notes match the schema from backend/app/agents/prd_critic.py.
